tldr: A mass crash inside the final 2 km tore the peloton apart, and Laurence Pithie sprinted clear of the survivors for Red Bull - BORA - hansgrohe to win in Vaux-sur-Sûre on a slight uphill drag to the line.

narrative.headline: Pithie wins a crash-shattered sprint
narrative.body: Stage 3 from Habay to Vaux-sur-Sûre (176.9 km) was a mostly flat day pointed at the sprinters and puncheurs, with a slight rise to the line making for a tricky finish. The day's breakaway — Jonah Killy, George Wood, Álvaro Sagrado, Filippo Conca, Jarno Bellens and Senne Thonnon — never threatened and was reeled in just outside the final 10 km. With every team saving its cards for a bunch sprint, a crash just outside the final kilometre split the peloton completely, leaving only a handful of riders at the front. Red Bull - BORA - hansgrohe had numbers there: Jordi Meeus and Danny van Poppel drove the lead-out and Laurence Pithie sprinted to victory, beating INEOS's Kim Heiduk to the line with Krists Neilands (NSN) third. All three picked up valuable bonus seconds for the overall.

gcImpact.headline: Heiduk moves into the lead as the GC tightens
gcImpact.body: The crash and the bonus seconds reshuffled a GC already separated by only a few seconds. Kim Heiduk took over the race lead heading into stage 4, holding a three-second margin over Jordi Meeus, Ben Oliver and Laurence Pithie — leaving the overall poised for the bonus-second skirmishes of the final two days.

riderPerformances.name: Jordi Meeus
riderPerformances.narrative: Rather than chasing the win himself, Meeus turned lead-out man, working with Danny van Poppel to deliver Laurence Pithie through the chaos of the crash-split finale. It was a selfless ride from the stage-1 winner that kept Red Bull - BORA - hansgrohe at the front of both the stage and the GC, with Meeus himself still inside three seconds of the new race leader.

keyMoments.what: Drove the Red Bull lead-out alongside van Poppel after a crash inside the final km split the bunch

crashes.description: A mass crash just outside the final kilometre brought down much of the peloton, splitting the bunch and leaving only a small front group to contest the sprint.

sources.title: Laurence Pithie sprints to win as mass crash takes down entire peloton
